description:
## What
Add `pinned_at INTEGER` (nullable) column to posts migration. Include `pinned: bool` field in all Status responses. Implement GET /api/v1/accounts/:id/statuses?pinned=true returning posts with pinned_at IS NOT NULL ordered by pinned_at DESC.

## Why
Clients display pinned posts prominently on profile pages; without the pinned field and endpoint, profile views are incomplete.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/accounts/:id/statuses?pinned=true on a known account with pinned posts; document JSON shape, ordering, and how the pinned bool appears on individual Status objects in other endpoints
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write the SQLite migration adding pinned_at column; update Status serializer to compute pinned bool as pinned_at IS NOT NULL; implement the ?pinned=true query path in the accounts statuses handler
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write tests that pin a post, fetch profile statuses with pinned=true, assert only pinned posts returned in pinned_at DESC order; also assert pinned field is false on normal status fetches

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social /api/v1/accounts/:id/statuses?pinned=true — returns array of Status objects, each with pinned: true, ordered by pinned_at descending
- Test approach: create multiple posts, pin two of them at different times, assert filtered endpoint returns only pinned posts in correct order

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- pinned_at is nullable — serializer must emit false (not null) for pinned bool when column is NULL
- Migration must allow NULL (no DEFAULT) so existing rows are correctly treated as unpinned
title: Add pinned_at column to posts and pinned field to Status; GET statuses?pinned=true
